untranslatable_reason: Signal requires direct measurement of implied volatility (IV) vs realized volatility (fair value estimation) and options-specific Greeks/pricing logic. Available indicators include hvol_20 (historical volatility) and india_vix (volatility index proxy), but the signal explicitly requires: (1) IV deviation quantification from fair value, (2) options vs outright derivatives selection logic, (3) conviction assessment metric. None of these can be constructed from the allowed price/technical indicators. The core edge (IV mispricing detection relative to realized vol) cannot be operationalized without implied volatility data.
translation_notes: Attempted proxy using hvol_20 vs india_vix crossover as volatility regime detector, but this does not capture the actual IV-vs-fair-value comparison required by Natenberg's principle. The signal is fundamentally an options pricing arbitrage strategy requiring volatility surface inputs unavailable in standard OHLCV backtest engines.
